results.notes: 1st gen Neon, shared with Dodge Neon
Factory staggered setup with different front/rear sizes
Plymouth Prowler was not produced in 1998. Production ran 1997, then 1999-2002.
Chrysler JA platform - shared with Cirrus/Stratus
All 90s Saturns use 4x100 with 57.1mm hub bore
Suzuki X-90 was discontinued after 1997 model year. No 1998 model produced.
Last year of Gen 1 Amigo (1995-1997 gap)

researchNotes:
Research completed for 7 gap makes (Plymouth, Saturn, Saab, Suzuki, Isuzu, Daewoo, Hummer). Key findings:

**Plymouth**: Most models used Chrysler 5x100 (57.1mm) platform. Voyager/Grand Voyager switched to 5x114.3 (71.5mm) in 1996. Laser uses DSM 5x114.3 (67.1mm). Prowler is staggered with 17" front/20" rear wheels. NOTE: 1998 Prowler did not exist (DNE).

**Saturn**: All S-series (SL/SC/SW) 1991-1999 use 4x100 with 57.1mm hub bore consistently.

**Saab**: 900 changed from 4x108 (classic 1987-1993) to 5x110 (NG900 1994-1998). 9000 maintained 4x108 throughout. All Saabs use 65.1mm hub bore. 9-3 and 9-5 (1999+) use 5x110.

**Suzuki**: Sidekick/X-90/Vitara/Grand Vitara all use 5x139.7 (108mm hub bore). Swift uses 4x114.3 (60.1mm). NOTE: X-90 did not exist in 1998 (DNE).

**Isuzu**: Most SUVs (Trooper/Rodeo/Amigo/VehiCROSS) use 6x139.7 with 100.5mm hub bore. Oasis is rebadged Honda Odyssey using 5x114.3 (64.1mm).

**Daewoo**: Lanos uses 4x100, Nubira uses 4x114.3, Leganza uses 5x114.3. All have ~56mm hub bore.

**Hummer H1**: Military-derived 8x165.1 (8x6.5") pattern with 117mm hub bore. Uses unique 16.5" runflat tires.

Sources used: tirewheelguide.com, hulkoffsets.com, roadkillcustoms.com, suzuki-forums.com, saabcentral.com, dsmtalk.com, flashoffroad.com, bbwheelsonline.com, avtoreference.com. NO wheel-size.com or banned retailer sites used.
